revision.comment: 新しいページ：三吉石塚古墳（奈良県広陵町にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三吉石塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（中央に後円部、右手前に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（中央群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]三吉 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 1.50 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 7.15 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長45m
高さ6.5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「三吉石塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-東 |ラベル = 三吉石塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''三吉石塚古墳'''（みつよしいしづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]三吉にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（うち中央群）を構成する古墳の1つ。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部、[[馬見丘陵]]中央部において[[新木山古墳]]（三吉陵墓参考地）の外堤西側に築造された古墳である。[[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘のうち後円部は2段築成。前方部の南東隅部には角状の張り出しを付す{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が検出されているほか、各段テラスにおいて[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が、墳頂部において[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・草摺形・短甲形・家形埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また墳丘周囲には馬蹄形（または盾形{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}）の周濠が巡らされる。主体部の埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 この三吉石塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。。馬見古墳群では、[[巣山古墳]]・[[乙女山古墳]]、[[新木山古墳]]・三吉石塚古墳、倉塚古墳・三吉古墳、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]・[[コンピラ山古墳]]のように大型前方後円墳と帆立貝形古墳（または大型円墳）が近接するという特徴が認められており、[[ヒメヒコ制]]との関連性を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）、町営墓地建設に伴う事前発掘調査（広陵町教育委員会、[[1988年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）3月6日、奈良県指定史跡に指定。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り三吉石塚古墳パンフレット。。 * 古墳総長：62メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：45メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：41.4メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：7メートル ** 幅：22メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル 墳丘の葺石には縦の列石が認められており、各列石間が作業単位とされる。また葺石に使用される石材のうち、後円部の輝石安山岩は香芝市の二上山麓産で、前方部の黒雲母花崗岩は當麻町西方産になる。 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む
{{small|葺石には作業単位が認められる。}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む
{{small|左後方は[[新木山古墳]]。}} == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三吉石塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）3月6日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（広陵町教育委員会、1995年設置） * 三吉石塚古墳パンフレット（広陵町教育委員会事務局文化財保存センター） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=三吉石塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 昭和62年度 石塚古墳 -範囲確認調査概報-・新木山古墳外堤 -範囲確認調査概報-（広陵町埋蔵文化財調査概報）]|publisher=広陵町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[新木山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 観光 古墳 > 三吉石塚古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:みつよしいしつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''三吉石塚古墳'''（みつよしいしづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]三吉にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（うち中央群）を構成する古墳の1つ。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部、[[馬見丘陵]]中央部において[[新木山古墳]]（三吉陵墓参考地）の外堤西側に築造された古墳である。[[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘のうち後円部は2段築成。前方部の南東隅部には角状の張り出しを付す{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が検出されているほか、各段テラスにおいて[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が、墳頂部において[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・草摺形・短甲形・家形埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また墳丘周囲には馬蹄形（または盾形{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}）の周濠が巡らされる。主体部の埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 この三吉石塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。。馬見古墳群では、[[巣山古墳]]・[[乙女山古墳]]、[[新木山古墳]]・三吉石塚古墳、倉塚古墳・三吉古墳、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]・[[コンピラ山古墳]]のように大型前方後円墳と帆立貝形古墳（または大型円墳）が近接するという特徴が認められており、[[ヒメヒコ制]]との関連性を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）、町営墓地建設に伴う事前発掘調査（広陵町教育委員会、[[1988年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）3月6日、奈良県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り三吉石塚古墳パンフレット。。 * 古墳総長：62メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：45メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：41.4メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：7メートル ** 幅：22メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル 墳丘の葺石には縦の列石が認められており、各列石間が作業単位とされる。また葺石に使用される石材のうち、後円部の輝石安山岩は香芝市の二上山麓産で、前方部の黒雲母花崗岩は當麻町西方産になる。 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む
{{small|葺石には作業単位が認められる。}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む
{{small|左後方は[[新木山古墳]]。}} == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三吉石塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）3月6日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（広陵町教育委員会、1995年設置） * 三吉石塚古墳パンフレット（広陵町教育委員会事務局文化財保存センター） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=三吉石塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 昭和62年度 石塚古墳 -範囲確認調査概報-・新木山古墳外堤 -範囲確認調査概報-（広陵町埋蔵文化財調査概報）]|publisher=広陵町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[新木山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 観光 古墳 > 三吉石塚古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:みつよしいしつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''三吉石塚古墳'''（みつよしいしづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]三吉にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（うち中央群）を構成する古墳の1つ。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部、[[馬見丘陵]]中央部において[[新木山古墳]]（三吉陵墓参考地）の外堤西側に築造された古墳である。[[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘のうち後円部は2段築成。前方部の南東隅部には角状の張り出しを付す{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が検出されているほか、各段テラスにおいて[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が、墳頂部において[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・草摺形・短甲形・家形埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また墳丘周囲には馬蹄形（または盾形{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}）の周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 この三吉石塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。。馬見古墳群では、[[巣山古墳]]・[[乙女山古墳]]、[[新木山古墳]]・三吉石塚古墳、倉塚古墳・三吉古墳、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]・[[コンピラ山古墳]]のように大型前方後円墳と帆立貝形古墳（または大型円墳）が近接するという特徴が認められており、[[ヒメヒコ制]]との関連性を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1987年]]度（[[昭和]]62年度）、町営墓地建設に伴う事前発掘調査（広陵町教育委員会、[[1988年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）3月6日、奈良県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り三吉石塚古墳パンフレット。。 * 古墳総長：62メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：45メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：41.4メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル * 前方部 - 1段築成。 ** 長さ：7メートル ** 幅：22メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル 墳丘の葺石には縦の列石が認められており、各列石間が作業単位とされる。また葺石に使用される石材のうち、後円部の輝石安山岩は香芝市の二上山麓産で、前方部の黒雲母花崗岩は當麻町西方産になる。 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む

{{small|左後方は[[新木山古墳]]。}} == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三吉石塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）3月6日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（広陵町教育委員会、1995年設置） * 三吉石塚古墳パンフレット（広陵町教育委員会事務局文化財保存センター） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=三吉石塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|三吉石塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 昭和62年度 石塚古墳 -範囲確認調査概報-・新木山古墳外堤 -範囲確認調査概報-（広陵町埋蔵文化財調査概報）]|publisher=広陵町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[新木山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 観光 古墳 > 三吉石塚古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:みつよしいしつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]] to [[Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

revision.text:
'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}

revision.text:
'''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Koryo, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} (a Reference Burial Mound for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): * Diameter: 41.4 meters * Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): * Length: 7 meters * Width: 22 meters * Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Nara Prefecture designated historic sites]] [[category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳}}
