revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.55041666666666|N|135.73531944444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Koryo, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hills|qq=}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (a Reference Kofun for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko system|qq=}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): ** Diameter: 41.4 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): ** Length: 7 meters ** Width: 22 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijo|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}
... 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara) ...l>''Miyoshi Ishizuka kofun'' || {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}} || || [[File:三吉石塚古墳 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka tumulus 2010.10.27 - panoramio.jpg|150px]] || {{coord|3 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{translated page|ja|三吉石塚古墳|version=99995446|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298215}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.55041666666666|N|135.73531944444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Koryo, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hills|qq=}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (a Reference Kofun for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko system|qq=}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): ** Diameter: 41.4 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): ** Length: 7 meters ** Width: 22 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijo|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}
... 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara) ...l>''Miyoshi Ishizuka kofun'' || {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}} || || [[File:三吉石塚古墳 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka tumulus 2010.10.27 - panoramio.jpg|150px]] || {{coord|3 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{translated page|ja|三吉石塚古墳|version=99995446|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298215}} [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko system|qq=}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijo|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun]][[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] [[ja:三吉石塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Koryo|qid=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.55041666666666|N|135.73531944444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|qid=Q11668062|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun''' (三吉石塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt=Koryo, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, Kitakatsuragi District, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} that forms part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (central group). The site has been designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is situated in the western part of Nara Prefecture, in the central area of the {{ill|Umami Hills|ja|馬見丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Hills|qq=}}, on the western outer embankment of the {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} (a Reference Kofun for Imperial Mausolea). It underwent an excavation survey in fiscal year 1987 (Showa 62). The kofun has a scallop-shaped design with a shortened front section, oriented eastward. The mound's rear circular portion is constructed in two tiers. The southeastern corner of the front section features a protruding angular design {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. Fukiishi (paving stones) were discovered on the mound's surface, and cylindrical haniwa rows, including asagao-shaped haniwa, were unearthed on each tier's terrace. Figurative haniwa (lid-shaped, kusazuri-shaped, tanko-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa) were found on the mound's summit {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}. A horseshoe-shaped (or shield-shaped {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}) moat surrounds the mound. The burial facility remains unexcavated, and no detailed information about grave goods is available. The Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{Sfn|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun (Heibonsha Revised Edition)|2006}}Interpretive board on-site.. In the Umami Kofun Cluster, large keyhole-shaped kofun and scallop-shaped or large circular kofun often appear in proximity, as seen with the {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|qid=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|qid=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}}, Arakiyama Kofun and Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, and others. This arrangement is theorized to relate to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko System|lt=Hime-Hiko system|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko system|qq=}} {{Sfn|Kunihiko Kawakami|2006}}. The kofun area was designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in 1992 (Heisei 4) and is now open to the public after conservation efforts. == Historical Background == * 1987 (Showa 62): A preliminary excavation survey was conducted prior to the construction of a municipal cemetery (reported by the Koryo Town Board of Education in 1988). * March 6, 1992 (Heisei 4): Designated as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as followsMitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun Pamphlet.: * Total length: 62 meters (including the moat) * Mound length: 45 meters * Rear circular portion (two tiers): ** Diameter: 41.4 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters * Front rectangular portion (one tier): ** Length: 7 meters ** Width: 22 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters The mound's fukiishi feature vertically aligned rows, with gaps between rows serving as work units. Stone materials for the paving differ by section: the pyroxene andesite in the rear circular portion originates from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijo|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, while the biotite granite in the front portion comes from the western part of Taima Town. File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular portion File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear circular portion from the front
{{small|The fukiishi demonstrate work-unit alignment.}} File:Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear
{{small|In the background: {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Designated on March 6, 1992{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf List of Nara Prefectural Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture official website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Not used in writing this article）}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/1771 Showa 62 Fiscal Year Ishizuka Kofun -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- & Arakiyama Kofun Outer Embankment -Scope Confirmation Survey Summary- (Koryo Town Cultural Properties Survey Summary)]|publisher=Koryo Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link to the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Archaeological Report Database." == See Also == * {{ill|Arakiyama Kofun|ja|新木山古墳|1=Nikiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=26 Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun - Koryo Town Official Website] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Mitsuyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|ja|三吉石塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}}
